records.ety: [L. instans, -antis, p. pr. of instare to stand upon, to press upon; pref. in- in, on + stare to stand: cf. F. instant. See Stand.]

quotes: Rejoicing in hope; patient in tribulation; continuing instant in prayer.
I am beginning to be very instant for some sort of occupation.

defs: Closely pressing or impending in respect to time; not deferred; immediate; without delay.
quotes: Impending death is thine, and instant doom.
The instant time is always the fittest time.
notes: ☞ The word in this sense is now used only in dates, to indicate the current month; as, the tenth of July instant.

quotes: Instant he flew with hospitable haste.

records.ety: [F. instant, fr. L. instans standing by, being near, present. See Instant, a.]

defs: A point in time; a moment; a portion of time too short to be estimated; also, any particular moment; as, the situation may change in an instant.
quotes: There is scarce an instant between their flourishing and their not being.

defs: A day of the present or current month; as, the sixth instant; -- an elliptical expression equivalent to the sixth of the month instant, i. e., the current month. See Instant, a., 3.
